mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index during inflationary periods indicate supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins and cause immediate negative market reactions.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in more than 50% of cases when industrial production declines during high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 2.5 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of months when CPI exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust job creation that boosts household income and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of observations over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity that flow through to broader corporate revenues and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year periods or housing starts show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low monetary policy rates signals accommodative financing for manufacturing expansion, triggering immediate positive market momentum.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances when industrial production rises alongside sub-1 percent policy rates.
